medications.dose_route_frequency: 1000mg orally every 6 hours (max 4g/day) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Short-term analgesia/anti-inflammatory for 48h; source advised 400mg three times daily with food.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 400mg orally three times daily with food for ~48 hours (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: rigid immobilisation / below-knee cast for a Grade 1 sprain
medications.indication_rationale: Rigid immobilisation of a stable Grade 1 sprain slows recovery; early protected mobilisation is preferred.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Early protected mobilisation — walk as pain allows; do ankle range-of-motion ('alphabet' with the toe) a few times daily; RICE for the first 48h.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Early movement reduces swelling, aligns collagen and speeds recovery vs immobilisation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Ice 15–20 min every 2h with a cloth barrier; compression (tubular bandage) and elevation.

patient_education_points.point: You've stretched (not torn) the ligament on the outside of your ankle, and the checks we did (you can weight-bear and there's no tenderness over the bones) mean an X-ray isn't needed. Because the joint is stable, keeping it moving actually heals it faster than resting it completely.
Walk as pain allows, do the toe-alphabet exercise a few times a day, and use ice/compression/elevation for the first couple of days. Expect recovery over 1–3 weeks.
Seek review if the foot becomes numb, cold or pale, if you become unable to bear any weight, or if severe swelling or worsening pain develops — then an X-ray may be needed.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Progress; physiotherapy for strengthening/proprioception; delayed X-ray/ultrasound if not improving by 7–10 days.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Numb/cold/pale foot, inability to bear any weight, or sudden severe swelling — ED/urgent care.

must_include_items: Apply the Ottawa Ankle Rules (weight-bearing + bony tenderness) to decide on imaging
Early protected mobilisation + RICE + range-of-motion (not rigid immobilisation)
acceptable_alternatives: Paracetamol and/or NSAID; tubular compression bandage; physiotherapy referral
errors_of_commission: Ordering an X-ray for an Ottawa-negative sprain
Advising rigid immobilisation / non-weight-bearing for a stable Grade 1 sprain
Prescribing opioids for a minor sprain
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires applying the Ottawa rules, withholding imaging when negative, and advising early protected mobilisation with a fracture safety-net.
